metadata.title: LSAT Writing Reference Library Registry
metadata.hybrid_mode: public app stays synthesized; local/mac app unlocks private reference library cards

resources.title: Companion Guide
resources.role: Full written manual aligned to the app sequence
resources.best_use: Read before a drill block or before rewriting a weak essay.
resources.summary: A structured written guide with formulas, drill instructions, and common mistake -> fix tables.

resources.title: Learning Module Companion Deck
resources.best_use: Skim before practice or use as a recap after a drill block.
resources.summary: A concise slide deck with one concept, one formula, one example, one drill prompt, and one strong-writer note per topic.

resources.title: Curriculum Overview
resources.role: Quick reference map of the whole system
resources.best_use: Use to orient yourself before a study session.
resources.summary: A compact map of the module order, sprint outputs, and timing rules.

resources.title: Curriculum Model JSON
resources.best_use: Use for implementation review, QA, or future automation.
resources.summary: The structured curriculum dataset that mirrors the deck, guide, and lesson order.

resources.title: Original Learning Module DOCX
resources.best_use: Read when expanding lesson explanations or checking the original drill sequencing.
resources.summary: Original learning-module writeup used to shape drill sequence, chunking, and explanation flow.

resources.title: Original Learning Module PPTX
resources.best_use: Use for slide framing, lesson summaries, and visual study recaps.
resources.summary: Original deck source used to strengthen slide-level explanation and quick review structure.

resources.title: Argumentative Notes from Sources
resources.best_use: Read before mechanism, thesis, or counter drills to gather phrasing and logic patterns.
resources.summary: Aggregated argumentative notes used to shape drills, sentence stems, and revision targets.

resources.title: LSAT Argumentative Writing Study Guide
resources.best_use: Read before official-mode or timed practice sessions.
resources.summary: Original guide that informed timing, prewriting structure, scratch-paper use, and full-essay flow.

resources.title: LSAT Practice Argumentative Essay
resources.best_use: Use while reviewing paragraph architecture and revision decisions.
resources.summary: Practice essay source used to reinforce outline sequencing and paragraph-role clarity.

resources.title: LSAT Argumentative Writing Notes
resources.best_use: Use for scope, thesis, and counterargument review before drilling.
resources.summary: Source notes that shaped the scope-box system, thesis formulas, and counter/limitation studio.

resources.title: Document2
resources.best_use: Use when diagnosing repeated weakness patterns and planning the next drill block.
resources.summary: Supplemental notes source used to reinforce revision patterns and coaching language.

resources.title: Document (6)
resources.best_use: Use while reviewing fallback examples and revision logic.
resources.summary: Supplemental notes that help with perspective synthesis, evidence meaning, and controlled revisions.

study_paths.title: Mechanism Path
study_paths.summary: Read the mechanism explanation first, then run a causal-chain drill before writing.

study_paths.title: Inference Path
study_paths.summary: Use the inference ladder to move from evidence to meaning to consequence.

study_paths.title: Counter + Limitation Path
study_paths.summary: Read how to steelman the objection, limit it, and return to comparison before timed rewriting.

study_paths.title: Thesis + Scope Path
study_paths.summary: Use the scope box and thesis formulas together before drafting any intro.

study_paths.title: Timed Performance Path
study_paths.summary: Review the timing rules, run a rep, then diagnose the single biggest weakness category.
